concerns.text: Phase 4 (US2) Implementation tasks (T023-T026) are listed AFTER their corresponding Test tasks (T020-T021). This violates the 'Producer before Consumer' and 'Tests fail before implementation' flow. The instrumentation code (producer) must exist before tests (consumer) can be written or executed. Reorder: Implementation tasks must precede Test tasks within the US2 block.

Phase 5 (US3) Implementation tasks (T029-T034) are listed AFTER their corresponding Test tasks (T027-T028). Similar to US2, the analysis logic (producer) must be implemented before unit tests (consumer) can be authored. Reorder: Implementation tasks must precede Test tasks within the US3 block.

Phase 6 (US4) Implementation tasks (T036-T039) are listed AFTER their corresponding Test tasks (T035). The sensitivity analysis logic (producer) must exist before tests (consumer) can validate it. Reorder: Implementation tasks must precede Test tasks within the US4 block.

Task T019.5 (Watchdog implementation in runner.py) is listed in Phase 3 (US1), but Task T030 (Time limit enforcement in runner.py) is in Phase 5 (US3). T030 explicitly states it uses 'watchdog logic from T019.5'. However, T029 (US3) depends on T019.5 being complete to run the training. While T019.5 is in Phase 3, the dependency chain is fragile. More critically, T019.5 is a cross-cutting concern (FR-004) that should ideally be in Phase 2 (Foundational) or explicitly marked as a prerequisite for T029. Currently, T029 (Phase 5) depends on T019.5 (Phase 3), which is acceptable, but T030 (Phase 5) re-implements or re-states the logic. T030 should be removed or clarified as 'Integrate T019.5' rather than 'Implement... using logic from', to avoid confusion on where the logic actually lives.

Task T013.2 (Generate mock history) is listed as a Test task (T013.2 [P] [US1] Generate...), but it is a data fixture required for T011, T012, T013. While fixtures are often part of the test suite, the task description 'Generate... data fixture' implies a setup step. Ensure T013.2 is explicitly ordered before T011, T012, T013 in the execution list, even if they are all in the 'Tests' section.
